problem.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
problem.description: Given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order, remove some duplicates in-place such that each unique element appears at most twice. The relative order of elements should be kept the same. Return k, the number of elements in the modified array.
assumptions: Array is sorted in non-decreasing order.
Each element may appear at most twice in the result.
Must be done in-place with O(1) extra space.

example.explanation: Allow at most 2 of each. Use a write pointer k: copy nums[i] to nums[k] only if k < 2 or nums[i] != nums[k-2] (meaning we haven't placed two of this value yet).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Use a write pointer k starting at 0. For each element in nums, write it to position k only if k < 2 or nums[i] != nums[k-2]. The condition nums[i] != nums[k-2] checks that we haven't already placed two copies of the current value — since the array is sorted, the value two positions back in the result array tells us whether we've hit the limit.
